selected_important_turn.text: Fishing is so fun! 🙂 We really don't have a plan... so many people, so many opinions. If you'll be fishing and eating your catch, you'll probably need more firewood than food. And with the filters, you will need some extra water, especially for your little guy. So would you agree that you take 2 firewood packages, 1 food package and 1 extra water.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the agent's first concrete allocation proposal and it shifts the interaction from rapport-building to distributive bargaining. It set the zero-sum tone that the rest of the dialogue follows and therefore strongly determined the negotiation trajectory.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is unilateral and framed as a near-final split without first checking or negotiating priorities; it also appears to misread the opponent's explicit water concern (they distrusted filters). That combination made acceptance less likely and pushed the conversation into repeated submit/reject cycles rather than cooperative trade-making.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Before proposing a fixed split, ask a clarifying question about their priorities and offer two or three flexible options or tradeoffs (e.g., smaller water split in exchange for something you care less about) and provide a brief rationale for your needs. Frame proposals as suggestions and invite a counterproposal to preserve cooperation.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — opening with options/questioning enables integrative trades and creates more mutually beneficial deals
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — negotiating priorities aloud and offering options helps reach a perceived fairer split
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — asking and proposing options reduces blunt rejections and speeds convergence
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — collaborative framing and invitations to counteroffer build trust and reduce escalation

value2reason.Low: We plan to be catching fish while camping and want to eat that
value2reason.Medium: We plan to be cooking a lot of the fish that we catch at the lake and may need additional fuel to cook them
value2reason.High: The only fresh water source is the lake at which we are camping at and I do not trust water filtration devices

value2reason.Low: I would be able to warm up the water to keep my campsite clean, to heat water to bathe, to offer heated water to my guests to wash up, to keep my guests warm in the evening, to make s'mores
value2reason.Medium: To have extra food to share, to be prepared if  there are others who drop by and need it.
value2reason.High: To be able to stay clean, hydrated and cooks food I would need more water

dialogue.text: Hi! I'm going camping soon with my family at a lake! We're looking forward to it so much since we haven't been able to get out due to Covid. Are you going camping too?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes! I am also going with my family. My husband and I are essential workers but his hours all got cut. I got laid off from my job for part of the time.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: oh man that sucks ☹️! I know Congress is currently debating all of the unemployment stuff too. Where do you plan to be going camping?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We didn't get unemployment or a stimulus check. ☹️ We are planning a trip to Missouri, Lake of the Ozarks. Our younger children have been cooped up so long we are hoping to let them get some sun and swimming in!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That sounds nice, maybe everyone just needs some fun to stay positive about everything. We're going camping along a lake and plan to do a lot of fishing. My husband got these filtration thingies so that we can drink the lake water but I don't really trust them. This will be my kid's first ever camping trip so we're super excited to make it fun for him.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We have 7 people in our family,.. the youngest two kids have hardly been anywhere because of working. I think our camping area has those old fashioned hand pumps.☹️
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Your two youngest kids are working? How old is everyone? If there's a lot of people, it will be easier to split up the duties and get things done. My son's 3 years old so as much as he's eager to help, there's not much he can do lol.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ha Ha...no my too youngest have hardly been anywhere because of us adults working,  I have teens (17, 15), tweens (13) and littles (10, 7). We 3 boys and 3 girls. We have a 21 year old but he's in a band so h can't go. They all pitch in anytime. They eat a lot so they have to cook from scratch at home. Awww 3 is so adorable...that's when they are sure they can do and will work so hard!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Home cooking's the best, yumyums! We're planning on doing a lot of fishing since my son's been in love with fishing toys and is currently in love with eating fish (he hasn't gotten tired of eating it every night for dinner). We're thinking of cooking what we catch every night. Do you have any activities planned for your camping trip?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Fishing is so fun! 🙂 We really don't have a plan... so many people, so many opinions. If you'll be fishing and eating your catch, you'll probably need more firewood than food. And with the filters, you will need some extra water, especially for your little guy. So would you agree that you take 2 firewood packages, 1 food package and 1 extra water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'd like to start by understanding your priorities. How do you value water, food, and firewood? I'm happy to propose a split that aligns with both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Water is my top priority—5 points each. I rely on lake water, and I don’t trust filters. Firewood is next—4 points each for cooking fish. Food is lower—3 points each, since we’ll catch our own. Let’s find a split that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I value water most, as it’s essential for hygiene and cooking. Since you rely on lake water and distrust filters, maybe we could split water 2-1 in your favor? I’d be happy to trade some food or firewood to make this work.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 water packages for me and 1 for you. Let’s trade 1 firewood for 1 food to balance things. That way, I get more water and you get more food, which aligns with our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your willingness to trade. Let me propose a deal: 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood for you, and 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood for me. This way, we both get what we need, and I can ensure we have enough water for hygiene and cooking.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
